A '''habitation sphere''' was a large artificial orbital structure designed to house large numbers of people.<ref name="TISWU" />

==History==
[[File:Habitation_spheres.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Giant-sized habitation spheres known as [[Worldcraft]] under construction in orbit of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].]]
Habitation spheres had been present around Coruscant since at least [[3640 BBY]].<ref name="TORA" /> This type of space station was known to orbit [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]] during the highpoint of the Galactic Empire. They were usually found in orbits above that of [[Skyhook/Legends|skyhooks]].<ref name="SOTESB">''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> One habitation sphere had a [[PIP/2 systems control droid]] installed aboard; the PIP/2 had a reputation for becoming overwhelmed and glitching and it cross wired the sphere's sewage lines to its [[Water/Legends|water]]-flow pipes. The incident resulted in 1,013 cases of severe intestinal distress.<ref name="EGTD">''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

According to a report by [[Pollux Hax/Legends|Pollux Hax]], two giant-sized habitation spheres were constructed in orbit of Coruscant by the planet's great space construction platforms, around the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="TISWU" /> The two belonged to a type of large habitation sphere-design known as [[Worldcraft]].<ref name="DOAE2" />

[[File:DS3-StarTours.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Alliance of Free Planets]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] attack a militarized habitation sphere near [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].]]
After the Battle of Endor, these two incomplete stations were moved by the Imperial splinter faction known as the [[Restored Empire]] and were used in its campaign initiated by [[Ennix Devian]].<ref name="DOAE2" /> [[Death Star III|One]] was sent to [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], the temporary headquarters of the [[Alliance of Free Planets]], as part of a feint maneuver. While the station [[Battle of Death Star III|was destroyed]],<ref name="ST">[[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']]</ref> Dissension troops stole warships from the Alliance's shipyards in neighboring systems.<ref name="DOAE2" />

The other sphere was sent to the [[Spawn Nebula/Legends|Spawn Nebula]] and turned into a repair facility/shipyard hub. Here, Devian gathered a fleet and armed many of his warships with [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] based on the main weapon of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] dreadnought [[Malevolence/Legends|''Malevolence'']].<ref name="DOAE2" /> The station, and much of the fleet, was later destroyed by an infiltration team armed with [[illerium]].<ref name="J8">{{JournalCite|8|Counterstrike (Adventure Journal)}}</ref><ref name="DOAE2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The artwork depicting the habitation spheres was originally concept art for an early story for [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], in which two [[Death Star/Legends|Death Stars]] were being built in orbit of the capital planet of [[Had Abbadon]]. As a result, one of the habitation spheres even has the recessed dish on its southern hemisphere where a [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] would be located.

The manner in which Pollux Hax discusses the spheres, insisting that they are being assembled "strictly for peaceful purposes," suggests the possibility that just the opposite is true—that these may have in fact been further Death Stars early in the process of being built. This supposition was often cited as a source for a [[Death Star III|third Death Star]] depicted in the [[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] ride, and was later confirmed to be the case in Part 2 of the StarWars.com Blogs series [[The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire]]—the blog post established the third Death Star as a [[worldcraft]], a type of habitation sphere.
